quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an implemented LLM tournament whose output is a ranking of candidates on rationale and literature quality.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: Reports concordance with human experts and intra-rater consistency, i.e. an agreement study against a human-preference reference rather than ground-truth property validation.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Physical assay data are routed back into the system and drive the next round of candidate generation, i.e. closed control rather than terminal assessment.

quotes.quote: Given a disease, Robin automatically identifies relevant in vitro assays that model key disease mechanisms and proposes specific drug candidates to evaluate in these experimental models.
quotes.rationale: Establishes that the evaluation environment is in vitro assays selected by the system.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Analysis of assay data is split between a human preprocessing step and an automated analysis component.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
quotes.rationale: Confirms the autonomous analysis step operates on human-supplied experimental data.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: A human, not the system, performs this evaluation of assay data, and the packet does not state how its output is routed.

quotes.quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
quotes.rationale: States that executable-protocol production, and hence any executability check on it, is an aspiration not yet realized; human translation currently bridges the gap.

diagnostics.consequence: Agreement with human preference cannot be coded as calibration or property-specific validation, so the ranking channel's ability to select experimentally successful candidates remains unestablished.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10
The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

diagnostics.statement: The only reliability study reported for the ranking channel compares the LLM judge with human expert preferences and measures intra-rater consistency; it does not validate ranking against experimental or ground-truth outcomes for the target property.

diagnostics.consequence: Human translation is an uninstrumented step between proposal and physical assay, so failures attributable to protocol specification cannot be separated from failures of the candidate hypothesis.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols

diagnostics.statement: The system produces experimental outlines rather than executable protocols, and no executability validator is reported for them.

diagnostics.consequence: The closure of the experimental feedback loop is overstated if human analysis contributions are attributed to the autonomous system; the human-analysis channel's feedback path must remain coded unclear.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

diagnostics.statement: Analysis of assay data is divided between automated and human steps, and the packet does not state how human-analyzed results are routed back into candidate generation.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time
Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report a validation of the LLM tournament ranking against measured assay outcomes for the ranked candidates, rather than only against expert preference agreement and self-consistency.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Top-ranked candidates outperform lower-ranked or randomly chosen candidates on the reported assay endpoint
success_check.evidence_needed: Reported assay results for candidates spanning the ranking, with ranks fixed before experiments
success_check.observable: Association between tournament rank and experimentally measured candidate effect in the in vitro assays

exact_quotes.exact_quote: future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution

recommendations.proposed_action: Instrument and report the human translation step from experimental outline to executed protocol so that protocol-specification failures are distinguishable from hypothesis failures.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every executed experiment has a reported outline-to-protocol delta
success_check.evidence_needed: Paired outline and executed-protocol records with annotated differences
success_check.observable: Documented record of edits made by humans when converting each outline into an executed protocol

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

recommendations.proposed_action: State explicitly, per analysis step, which outputs are produced by humans versus automated components and whether each is routed back into the next candidate round.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: No analysis step in the reported loop lacks a stated performer and routing destination
success_check.evidence_needed: A reported loop diagram or table listing each analysis step, its performer, and its consumer
success_check.observable: Per-analysis attribution of actor and downstream routing

policy_type: LLM-judged tournament ranking of drug candidates
iterative candidate rounds conditioned on returned experimental data
